Caption: Schematic illustration of (a) the extrusion process used to fabricate the Mg–Ca alloy and the dimensions of the disc-shaped specimens, and (b) the surface pretreatment procedures applied to the Mg–Ca alloy prior to coating, including mechanical polishing and in situ Ar+ etching.
Schematic of the coaxial arc plasma deposition (CAPD) method, representative plasma pulses during deposition, and Q-Dia films on Mg–Ca alloy samples prepared with different surface pretreatments.

Schematic diagrams of (a) the three-electrode electrochemical cell for corrosion testing in SBF and (b) the tribological test configuration using an Al2O3 ball sliding against bare and Q-Dia-coated Mg–Ca alloy surfaces under SBF lubrication.

Top-view SEM images of bare Mg–Ca alloy under (a) mechanical polishing treatment and (b) Ar + etching treatment. Q-Dia coatings deposited on Mg–Ca alloy: (c, e) Q-Dia1 coating with surface pretreatment by polishing, and (d, f) Q-Dia2 coating with surface pretreatment by Ar etching.
a) Cross-sectional SEM images and b) cross-sectional EDS scan of Q-Dia film on Mg–Ca alloy. Surface EDS spectra of c) bare Mg–Ca alloy and d) Q-Dia film on Mg–Ca alloy.
Surface roughness of bare Mg–Ca substrate under a) mechanical polishing and b) Ar+ etching treatment measured by 3D laser confocal microscopy.

AFM surface morphologies and corresponding three-dimensional topographic images of quench-produced diamond coatings deposited on Mg–Ca alloys: (a) Q-Dia1 coating prepared on mechanically polished substrate and (b) Q-Dia2 coating prepared on Ar-ion-etched substrate.
X-ray photoelectron spectra of a) bare Mg–Ca alloy and b) Q-Dia film. c) High-resolution C 1s XPS spectrum and d) Raman spectrum of the Q-Dia film.
Open circuit potential evolution of bare Mg–Ca alloy, Q-Dia1 and Q-Dia2 immersed in a SBF solution.
Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy results in SBF solution: (a) Nyquist plots; (b, c) Bode plots of the bare Mg–Ca alloy, Q-Dia1, and Q-Dia2 films.
Equivalent circuit models used for fitting the EIS data of a) the bare Mg–Ca alloy and b) Q-Dia films.
Polarization resistance of the bare Mg–Ca alloy, Q-Dia1, and Q-Dia2 films.
Potentiodynamic polarization results in SBF solution: (a) polarization curves and (b) corrosion rate of the bare Mg–Ca alloy, Q-Dia1, and Q-Dia2 films.
A schematic of the corrosion and protection mechanism in SBF solution for (a–c) Q-Dia1 and (d–f) Q-Dia2 coatings on Mg–Ca alloy.

Scratch test results of the Q-Dia2 coating on the Mg–Ca alloy: (a) Optical image of the scratch track with tangential force as a function of scratch length and the corresponding critical loads (LC2 and LC3); (b) normal force, acoustic emission, and penetration depth signals recorded during the scratch test.

Tribological behavior under wet sliding conditions in SBF: (a) friction curves; (b) average coefficient of friction (COF) and wear rate of the of the Al2O3 counter-body; (c, d) wear track morphologies of the bare Mg–Ca alloy and the Q-Dia2-coated sample tested against an Al2O3 counter-body under normal loads of 2 N and 4 N for 10 min.
